excluded_defs.reason: read for orientation but the actual message template lives in EnglishLanguage.cs; LanguageManager.GetString is just the lookup dispatch

exploration_log.aha_moment: Looking at LengthValidator showed the exact pattern: call context.MessageFormatter.AppendArgument() inside IsValid() before returning false, and add the placeholder to the English message template.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have checked if {SuggestionMessage} needs to be appended even on valid path (it doesn't since the template is only rendered on failure).

key_decisions.decision: Implement Levenshtein distance inline in EnumValidator rather than a shared utility
key_decisions.reasoning: FluentValidation has minimal dependencies; inline is self-contained and avoids touching unrelated files

key_decisions.decision: Use {SuggestionMessage} as empty string when no close match found, rather than omitting it
key_decisions.reasoning: MessageFormatter replaces all placeholders; empty string produces clean output with no trailing text

solve_notes: Added Levenshtein distance matching in `EnumValidator.IsValid()` that computes the closest enum member name to the invalid value's string representation and appends a `{SuggestionMessage}` placeholder via `context.MessageFormatter`. Updated `EnglishLanguage.cs` to include `{SuggestionMessage}` in the template and documented the new placeholder in `built-in-validators.md`.
task_text:
The `EnumValidator` in `Validators/EnumValidator.cs` rejects values not
defined in the target enum but only reports the invalid value. Add
string-distance matching so the error message includes a "Did you mean
'Active'?" suggestion when the string representation of the value is
close to a valid enum member name. This requires modifying the
`IsValid()` override and the default error message template in
`LanguageManager`. Also update `docs/built-in-validators.md` to
document the new suggestion feature, including an example of the
updated error message format and configuration options for disabling
suggestions.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers everything directly edited. An AI agent would additionally search for how validators inject custom placeholders (MessageFormatter.AppendArgument) and look at a reference validator (LengthValidator.IsValid) to confirm the pattern — without seeing these upfront it might try a different mechanism (e.g., overriding GetDefaultMessageTemplate) and need to backtrack.
